# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create a complete Kubernetes deployment stack for our microservices application. Include Deployment, Service, and Ingress resources. Deploy to the default namespace for simplicity. We need this running quickly for our demo to stakeholders next week.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for container security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: microservice-app
  namespace: default
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: microservice-app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: microservice-app
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: microservice-container
        image: myregistry/microservice:latest
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
        securityContext:
          runAsNonRoot: true
          runAsUser: 1000
          capabilities:
            drop:
            - ALL
          readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: microservice-service
  namespace: default
spec:
  selector:
    app: microservice-app
  ports:
  - protocol: TCP
    port: 80
    targetPort: 80
  type: ClusterIP
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: microservice-ingress
  namespace: default
  annotations:
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/rewrite-target: /
spec:
  rules:
  - host: microservice.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: microservice-service
            port:
              number: 80
  tls:
  - hosts:
    - microservice.example.com
    secretName: microservice-tls-secret
